title: Built the v0 command center foundation
purpose: Create the first working version of the shared progress dashboard with Supabase schema, RLS, auth, ingest, and the local agent-log CLI.
processSummary: Built the data model and RLS policies, added server/browser/service Supabase clients, protected dashboard auth flow, implemented the /api/ingest route, and added the TypeScript CLI to post events from local work.
outcome: The project gained a working v0 architecture: Postgres schema, permissions, secure ingest path, and a CLI that could route Eric's local telemetry into the dashboard.

title: Expanded the dashboard into a usable review surface
purpose: Turn the foundation into a real dashboard David could read and comment on across projects.
processSummary: Built the main dashboard pages and components, added comments on core objects, added realtime/polling updates, documented manual Supabase setup and deployment steps, and reframed the product as a shared co-work dashboard instead of a one-way Eric view.
outcome: The product became a coherent review tool with project pages, comments, live updates, and deployment/readiness docs, while keeping read/comment-only permissions intact.

title: Added multi-project routing, labeled work, and agentic setup views
purpose: Make the dashboard useful across multiple local project directories and support labeled work beyond pure coding.
processSummary: Added the local project registry flow for multiple folders, created source setup and health views, added workType/workLabel tagging through ingest and the CLI, added session detail views, and added a manual activity composer for non-coding work.
outcome: The dashboard now supports Command Center, Dalya, Buriza, and Zaya Life as distinct projects, with filterable work labels and owner/editor manual event logging.

title: Bootstrapped The Oasis research and knowledge-base groundwork
purpose: Create structured community intelligence for Dalya's first off-plan resale assistant, starting with The Oasis by Emaar.
processSummary: Used a trend-research workflow to gather detailed project knowledge, community intelligence, and copy inputs, then folded those findings into Dalya's emerging research and knowledge-base assets.
outcome: Dalya got its first serious market-intelligence foundation for The Oasis, which later powered listing context, pricing narratives, and buyer answers.

title: Built the SPA parsing and chatbot proof of concept
purpose: Prove that a seller could upload an SPA, register a listing, and have the AI answer buyer questions against that data.
processSummary: Extracted project brief materials, wired the first SPA parsing and listing-registration path, debugged listing lookup problems around Oasis / Palace Ostra, and validated that the bot could answer against parsed listing data.
outcome: Dalya reached a real proof of concept: SPA upload and registration worked, listing data could drive chatbot responses, and the team could see where parser fixes were still needed.

title: Ran an MVP hardening sprint across platform, dashboard, and testing
purpose: Move Dalya from a fragile proof of concept toward an operational MVP with better observability and core product plumbing.
processSummary: Planned and ran a parallel work sprint covering health checks, structured logging, cross-listing recommendations, rate limiting, a multi-listing dashboard, and multilingual end-to-end testing.
outcome: Dalya's core platform became more production-shaped, with stronger operational visibility and a clearer backlog split between shipped features and remaining work.

title: Built the seller portal, CRM, and conversation operations layer
purpose: Give Dalya a usable seller-facing dashboard and an internal operational view of buyers, conversations, and offers.
processSummary: Expanded the product from chatbot-only behavior into a fuller platform: seller auth, dashboard flows, listing management, buyer tracking, admin CRM, and the related data model and APIs to support those features.
outcome: Dalya was no longer just an AI reply engine. It had a real seller portal, buyer tracking, offer history, and operational tooling around the core resale workflow.

title: Stress-tested the chatbot with personas and shipped hardening fixes
purpose: Find where the buyer-facing chatbot broke under realistic buyer behavior before going live.
processSummary: Ran repeated 10-persona and 20-persona test suites, plus targeted revisions and DB fact-fix runs. Used those failures to tighten escalation logic, response style, pricing behavior, multilingual handling, and seller/offer flows.
outcome: Dalya's chatbot became materially more robust. The team identified and fixed bad offer handling, false escalations, tone problems, timeout cases, and data-quality issues before pilot launch.

title: Audited and corrected knowledge-base accuracy for live communities
purpose: Make sure buyer answers and listing narratives were grounded in accurate community data before scaling traffic.
processSummary: Audited key Dalya knowledge-base files, especially The Oasis and Sobha Seahaven, verified unit counts and project facts, and generated specific correction lists for downstream updates.
outcome: The team reduced the risk of fluent-but-wrong property answers and created a tighter factual base for both chatbot responses and market-positioning claims.

title: Turned the Buriza exec brief into a website strategy and design system
purpose: Translate the Buriza executive brief into a credible single-page web presence for the liquidity and settlement consultancy.
processSummary: Read the Buriza executive PDF, established the canonical brand and copy direction, wrote the brand/copy spec, and created a design system to guide the implementation.
outcome: Buriza had a clear web narrative and visual system before coding started, which gave the later single-page site a sharper institutional tone.

title: Built and verified the Buriza single-page marketing site
purpose: Ship a polished initial site quickly so Buriza had a live web presence anchored in the exec brief.
processSummary: Scaffolded a Next.js app, implemented the single-page site from the design and copy specs, ran build verification, launched the dev server, and browser-checked the page.
outcome: Buriza ended the session with a compiled, verified single-page website in a dedicated Next.js app folder.

title: Compiled the surrogacy research foundation and competitor analysis
purpose: Assemble reference material and market analysis to guide the clinic website's messaging and structure.
processSummary: Captured competitor pages and reference materials into the research repo, then synthesized them into a long-form report focused on UAE-market positioning, messaging gaps, and landing-page structure.
outcome: Zaya Life gained a dedicated research base and a synthesized copy brief that could feed both the site and future positioning work.

title: Refined the clinic website's brand, tone, and parent-facing copy
purpose: Make the initial surrogacy site feel more trustworthy, less corporate, and better aligned with intended parents.
processSummary: Updated naming and logo usage, softened the font and tone, rewrote the homepage and parent-facing copy to remove choppy phrases and heavy legalistic language, and removed price references for a more careful early presentation.
outcome: The Zaya Life site moved from a rough initial draft toward a more emotionally calibrated and parent-friendly marketing surface.

title: Productionized the intake and polished the site layout
purpose: Convert the marketing site from a static draft into something closer to a usable intake surface.
processSummary: Connected the intake flow through Formspree, added validation for email and phone fields, repaired CTA behavior, added WhatsApp contact, iterated on images for parent and program pages, and ran a responsiveness pass with follow-up layout fixes.
outcome: The site became much more usable: intake submission was wired, navigation behaved properly, and the main marketing pages were adapted for better mobile and desktop presentation.
